title: Charmed Life - Chapter 22 (Fragment 1)
text:
Types of Magic

T YPES OF M AGIC

WHAT IS MAGIC ? There is no easy answer to this question. For in the worlds of Chrestomanci, magic is as individual as the person using it. And if two people use the same spell but the results are different, this may not only be due to who is doing the magic, but how they are doing the magic, and whether the magic is being used for good or evil.

Is it possible to learn magic? If no one in your family has ever shown signs of magical ability, it is very unlikely that you will be able to do magic. If, however, your great-aunt twice removed once turned herself invisible, the chances are there is magic in your family and you may well have inherited it. It is no surprise that Roger and Julia Chant can do magic in Charmed Life , for example, given that their father is Chrestomanci. Even those children from a magical family who seem to have no magic ability at all usually find there’s a logical reason why not, as magic will out!

But being born with magical ability is not enough. Magic users are not born knowing spells and charms — these have to be learnt. Spell-making and spell-using is handed down like recipes through generations in The Magicians of Caprona , where each spell-making family is a school of learning. In The Lives of Christopher Chant , Charmed Life , and Mixed Magics , alongside lessons on history, geography, and mathematics, the student has to take a series of Elementary and Advanced Magic classes. It is only when you pass the Advanced Magic classes that you become what is known as an “adept.”

So what happens in a world where magic and witchcraft are outlawed? Larwood House, the school for witch-orphans in Witch Week , is the very last place you would have magic lessons, for magic is utterly forbidden. Still, the funny thing about magic is that, lessons or no lessons, it has a habit of breaking out like measles, whether in the form of a swarm of exotic birds, a flying mop, or a running shoe disguised as a gateau. One thing is certain: although erratic, the results of untrained magic can be quite spectacular.

vocabulary.definition: Possession of the means or skill to do something.
vocabulary.example: Magical ability is rare.

vocabulary.definition: A person highly skilled or proficient at something.
vocabulary.example: It is only when you pass Advanced Magic classes that you become an adept.

vocabulary.definition: Most important or main part of something.
vocabulary.example: Words central to the fragment are chosen.

vocabulary.definition: The likelihood of something happening.
vocabulary.example: There is a chance that magic runs in your family.

vocabulary.definition: A small object, or spell, believed to have magical powers.
vocabulary.example: Magic users are not born knowing spells and charms.

vocabulary.definition: Not the same as another or each other.
vocabulary.example: The results of the same spell may be different.

vocabulary.definition: Irregular, unpredictable, or inconsistent in movement or behaviour.
vocabulary.example: Although erratic, the results can be spectacular.

vocabulary.definition: A group consisting of parents and their children living together in a household.
vocabulary.example: Their father is Chrestomanci, so their family has magic.

vocabulary.word: family who seem to have no magic ability
vocabulary.lemma: family who seem to have no magic ability
vocabulary.ipa: ˈfæmɪli huː siːm tu hæv nəʊ ˈmædʒɪk əˈbɪləti

translations.ru: семья, члены которой, кажется, не обладают магическими способностями

vocabulary.definition: A group related by blood where individuals apparently lack magical power.
vocabulary.example: Even those children from a magical family who seem to have no magic ability usually find there’s a logical reason why not.

vocabulary.definition: Used to introduce something chosen as a typical case.
vocabulary.example: For example, Roger and Julia can do magic.

vocabulary.definition: Not allowed; banned.
vocabulary.example: Magic is utterly forbidden in Larwood House.

vocabulary.definition: To pass from one person or generation to another.
vocabulary.example: Spells are handed down like recipes through generations.

vocabulary.definition: Single; separate or distinct from others.
vocabulary.example: Magic is as individual as the person using it.

vocabulary.definition: To receive something from someone after their death or from ancestors.
vocabulary.example: You may well have inherited magical ability.

vocabulary.definition: Received something (especially a characteristic or possession) from a predecessor.
vocabulary.example: You may well have inherited it from your family.

vocabulary.definition: Unable to be seen.
vocabulary.example: She turned herself invisible.

vocabulary.definition: A period of learning or teaching about a subject.
vocabulary.example: Magic lessons are offered in some schools.

vocabulary.definition: Reasonable and sensible.
vocabulary.example: Usually there’s a logical reason for why someone has no magic ability.

vocabulary.definition: A class or session for teaching magic skills and knowledge.
vocabulary.example: Larwood House is the last place you would have magic lessons.

vocabulary.definition: The power of apparently influencing events using mysterious or supernatural forces.
vocabulary.example: Magic can be used for good or evil.

vocabulary.definition: A family known for having members with magical abilities.
vocabulary.example: Children from a magical family usually have some magic ability.

vocabulary.definition: Declared illegal or forbidden by law or authority.
vocabulary.example: Magic and witchcraft are outlawed in that world.

vocabulary.definition: Impressive to look at or very dramatic.
vocabulary.example: The results of untrained magic can be quite spectacular.

vocabulary.definition: A form of words used as a magical charm or incantation.
vocabulary.example: Two people use the same spell but the results differ.
